lessons.name: Unit 7: The World of Mass Media
lessons.chapter: Unit 7: The World of Mass Media
lessons.description: Learn about mass media, its types and influence, and adverbial clauses of manner and result.

theory.title: Unit 7: The World of Mass Media

sections.heading: 1. Từ vựng (Vocabulary)
sections.content:
WordType & IPAMeaningExample
Mass media(n) /mæs ˈmiːdiə/Phương tiện truyền thông đại chúngMass media influences public opinion.
Broadcast(v, n) /ˈbrɔːdkɑːst/Phát sóngThe news is broadcast daily.
Journalism(n) /ˈdʒɜːnəlɪzəm/Nghề báo chíShe studied journalism.
Journalist(n) /ˈdʒɜːnəlɪst/Nhà báoThe journalist reported the news.
Headline(n) /ˈhedlaɪn/Tiêu đềThe headline caught attention.
Article(n) /ˈɑːtɪkl/Bài báoI read an interesting article.
Audience(n) /ˈɔːdiəns/Khán giảThe show attracted a large audience.
Viewer(n) /ˈvjuːə(r)/Người xemMillions of viewers watched.
Subscriber(n) /səbˈskraɪbə(r)/Người đăng kýThe channel has many subscribers.
Influence(v, n) /ˈɪnfluəns/Ảnh hưởngMedia influences opinions.
Bias(n) /ˈbaɪəs/Thiên kiếnThe report showed bias.
Reliable(adj) /rɪˈlaɪəbl/Đáng tin cậyThis is a reliable source.
Fake news(n) /feɪk njuːz/Tin giảFake news spreads quickly.
Viral(adj) /ˈvaɪrəl/Lan truyền nhanhThe video went viral.
Report(v, n) /rɪˈpɔːt/Đưa tinReporters report the news.

sections.heading: 2. Luyện tập từ vựng (Vocabulary Practice)
sections.content:
Types of Mass Media:

  • Print media: newspapers, magazines, books
  • Broadcast media: TV, radio
  • Digital media: websites, social media, blogs
  • Social media: Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, TikTok

People in Media:

  • journalist: nhà báo
  • reporter: phóng viên
  • editor: biên tập viên
  • photographer: nhiếp ảnh gia
  • broadcaster: người phát thanh
  • influencer: người có ảnh hưởng
  • content creator: người tạo nội dung

Media Content:

  • news: tin tức
  • headline: tiêu đề
  • article: bài báo
  • report: bản tin
  • interview: phỏng vấn
  • documentary: phim tài liệu
  • advertisement: quảng cáo
  • post: bài đăng
  • comment: bình luận

Media Actions:

  • broadcast: phát sóng
  • publish: xuất bản
  • report: đưa tin
  • share: chia sẻ
  • post: đăng bài
  • like: thích
  • comment: bình luận
  • subscribe: đăng ký
  • follow: theo dõi
  • go viral: lan truyền nhanh

Media Issues:

  • fake news: tin giả
  • misinformation: thông tin sai lệch
  • bias: thiên kiến
  • censorship: kiểm duyệt
  • privacy: quyền riêng tư
  • cyberbullying: bắt nạt trên mạng

sections.heading: 3. Ngữ âm: Nhấn mạnh trong mệnh đề trạng ngữ
sections.content:
Stress in adverbial clauses

Quy tắc nhấn mạnh:

1. Mệnh đề trạng ngữ chỉ cách thức (Manner):

  • Nhấn mạnh động từ chính và cách thức
  • Ex: She reported the news AS IF she was THERE.
  • Ex: He speaks AS THOUGH he KNOWS everything.

2. Mệnh đề trạng ngữ chỉ kết quả (Result):

  • Nhấn mạnh mức độ và kết quả
  • Ex: The news was SO SHOCKING that everyone TALKED about it.
  • Ex: She has SUCH INFLUENCE that millions FOLLOW her.

3. Lưu ý:

  • Liên từ (as if, as though, so...that, such...that) thường nhấn nhẹ
  • Tính từ/trạng từ sau 'so' được nhấn mạnh
  • Danh từ sau 'such' được nhấn mạnh

sections.heading: 4. Ngữ pháp: Mệnh đề trạng ngữ chỉ cách thức và kết quả (Adverbial Clauses of Manner and Result)
sections.content:
A. Mệnh đề trạng ngữ chỉ cách thức (Adverbial Clauses of Manner)

Định nghĩa: Mệnh đề trạng ngữ chỉ cách thức mô tả cách thức một hành động được thực hiện.

1. AS IF / AS THOUGH (như thể):

  • Cấu trúc: S + V + as if/as though + S + V
  • Dùng để diễn tả tình huống không có thật
  • Với hiện tại: as if/as though + Past Simple
  • Ex: She talks as if she knew everything.
  • Với quá khứ: as if/as though + Past Perfect
  • Ex: He spoke as though he had been there.

2. AS (như):

  • Cấu trúc: S + V + as + S + V
  • Dùng để mô tả cách thức thực tế
  • Ex: Do as I say.
  • Ex: She reported the news as it happened.

3. THE WAY (cách mà):

  • Cấu trúc: S + V + the way + S + V
  • Ex: I like the way she presents the news.

B. Mệnh đề trạng ngữ chỉ kết quả (Adverbial Clauses of Result)

1. SO...THAT (quá...đến nỗi):

  • Cấu trúc: S + V + so + adj/adv + that + S + V
  • Dùng với tính từ hoặc trạng từ
  • Ex: The news was so shocking that everyone talked about it.
  • Ex: The video went viral so quickly that millions saw it.
  • So many/much + noun + that:
  • Ex: There was so much fake news that people didn't know what to believe.

2. SUCH...THAT (quá...đến nỗi):

  • Cấu trúc: S + V + such + (a/an) + adj + noun + that + S + V
  • Dùng với danh từ
  • Ex: It was such shocking news that everyone talked about it.
  • Ex: She is such a good journalist that everyone trusts her.

C. So sánh SO và SUCH

  • SO + adj/adv: so beautiful, so quickly
  • SUCH + noun phrase: such a beautiful day, such quick action

sections.heading: 5. I. Getting Started
sections.content:
Chủ đề: Mass media in modern life

Nội dung chính:

  • Hội thoại về các loại phương tiện truyền thông
  • Thảo luận về ảnh hưởng của truyền thông
  • Chia sẻ về thói quen tiêu thụ tin tức
  • Nói về tin giả và độ tin cậy

sections.heading: 6. II. Language
sections.content:
A. Pronunciation: Stress in adverbial clauses

B. Vocabulary: Mass Media

  • Types: print, broadcast, digital, social media
  • People: journalist, reporter, editor, broadcaster
  • Content: news, headline, article, report
  • Actions: broadcast, publish, share, go viral
  • Issues: fake news, bias, misinformation

C. Grammar: Adverbial Clauses of Manner and Result

  • Manner: as if/as though, as, the way
  • Result: so...that, such...that

sections.heading: 7. III. Reading
sections.content:
Chủ đề: The influence of mass media

Kỹ năng:

  • Đọc hiểu về ảnh hưởng của truyền thông
  • Tìm thông tin về các loại phương tiện
  • Hiểu về vai trò của truyền thông
  • Phân tích tác động tích cực và tiêu cực

sections.heading: 8. IV. Speaking
sections.content:
Chủ đề: Discussing mass media and its impact

Useful expressions:

  • I get my news from...
  • Social media is so popular that...
  • The news spread so quickly that...
  • Some journalists report as if they were there.
  • Fake news is such a problem that...

sections.heading: 9. V. Listening
sections.content:
Chủ đề: A talk about social media

Kỹ năng:

  • Nghe hiểu bài nói về mạng xã hội
  • Điền thông tin về ảnh hưởng của social media
  • Trả lời câu hỏi về lợi ích và thách thức

sections.heading: 10. VI. Writing
sections.content:
Chủ đề: Writing an article about mass media

Kỹ năng:

  • Viết bài báo về truyền thông
  • Trình bày ảnh hưởng của media
  • Sử dụng mệnh đề trạng ngữ
  • Đưa ra quan điểm và lập luận

sections.heading: 11. VII. Communication and Culture / CLIL
sections.content:
Communication: Discussing news and media

Culture: Media around the world

  • USA: Freedom of press
  • UK: BBC, tabloid culture
  • China: State-controlled media
  • Vietnam: Growing digital media

CLIL (Media Studies): How media works

  • News gathering and reporting
  • Editorial process
  • Media bias and objectivity
  • Fact-checking and verification

sections.heading: 12. VIII. Looking Back
sections.content:
Ôn tập:

  • Pronunciation: Stress in adverbial clauses
  • Vocabulary: Mass media, broadcast, journalism, journalist, headline, article, audience, viewer, influence, bias, reliable, fake news, viral
  • Grammar: Adverbial clauses of manner (as if/as though, as, the way), Result (so...that, such...that)

sections.heading: 13. Project
sections.content:
Đề tài: Create a media analysis project

Nhiệm vụ:

  • Chọn một sự kiện tin tức gần đây
  • So sánh cách các phương tiện khác nhau đưa tin
  • Phân tích độ tin cậy và thiên kiến
  • Tạo presentation
  • Trình bày trước lớp (5-7 phút)

problemTypes.name: Type 1: Adverbial Clauses
problemTypes.method: Use adverbial clauses of manner and result correctly.

exercises.content:
Read the following passage and answer the questions that follow.

Mass media has become such a powerful force in modern society that it shapes how we think, what we believe, and how we behave. From traditional newspapers and television to digital platforms and social media, mass media influences nearly every aspect of our lives.

The rise of social media has been so dramatic that it has transformed how people communicate and consume information. Platforms like Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram have billions of users who share news, opinions, and personal updates. Information spreads so quickly on social media that a story can reach millions of people within hours. However, this speed comes with risks - fake news and misinformation can go viral just as fast as accurate information.

Traditional media like newspapers and television still play important roles, though their influence has declined. Many people now get their news from online sources because digital media is so convenient that they can access it anytime, anywhere. Journalists report news as if they are providing objective facts, but media bias exists in both traditional and digital platforms. Some news outlets present information in such a way that it supports their political or commercial interests.

The challenge for modern citizens is developing media literacy - the ability to critically evaluate information. We should not believe everything we read or watch as though it were automatically true. Instead, we need to check multiple sources, verify facts, and think critically about what we consume. The media's influence is so strong that it can shape public opinion and even affect elections, so being an informed and critical consumer of media is more important than ever.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
questions.option_a: Social media is better than traditional media
questions.option_b: Mass media is powerful and requires critical thinking
questions.explanation: Đoạn văn nói về sức mạnh của truyền thông đại chúng và nhu cầu tư duy phản biện.

questions.question: Why does information spread quickly on social media?
questions.option_b: Because of the nature and reach of social platforms
questions.explanation: Thông tin lan nhanh do bản chất và phạm vi tiếp cận của các nền tảng mạng xã hội.

questions.question: The word 'objective' in paragraph 3 is closest in meaning to ______.
questions.explanation: Objective = unbiased and factual (khách quan, không thiên vị).

questions.question: What problem is mentioned about fast information spread?
questions.option_b: Fake news can spread as quickly as real news
questions.explanation: Vấn đề là tin giả có thể lan nhanh như tin thật.

questions.question: According to the passage, what is media literacy?
questions.option_b: The ability to critically evaluate information
questions.explanation: Media literacy là khả năng đánh giá thông tin một cách phản biện.

exercises.question: The news was ______ shocking ______ everyone talked about it.
exercises.explanation: So...that - mệnh đề kết quả với tính từ: so + adj + that.

exercises.question: She talks ______ she knew everything.
exercises.explanation: As if - mệnh đề cách thức: như thể (giả định không có thật).

exercises.question: It was ______ interesting article ______ I shared it.
exercises.explanation: Such an...that - mệnh đề kết quả với danh từ đếm được số ít.

exercises.question: He reported the news ______ he had been there.
exercises.explanation: As though - mệnh đề cách thức: như thể (giả định quá khứ).

exercises.question: Social media is ______ popular ______ billions use it.
exercises.explanation: So...that - mệnh đề kết quả với tính từ.

exercises.question: The video went viral ______ quickly ______ millions saw it.
exercises.explanation: So...that - mệnh đề kết quả với trạng từ.

exercises.question: She acts ______ she were famous.
exercises.explanation: As if - mệnh đề cách thức với 'were' (giả định không có thật).

exercises.question: There was ______ much fake news ______ people didn't know what to believe.
exercises.explanation: So much...that - mệnh đề kết quả với danh từ không đếm được.

exercises.question: Do ______ the journalist says.
exercises.explanation: As - mệnh đề cách thức: theo cách, như (thực tế).

exercises.question: She has ______ influence ______ millions follow her.
exercises.explanation: Such...that - mệnh đề kết quả với danh từ.

exercises.question: I like ______ she presents the news.
exercises.explanation: The way - mệnh đề cách thức: cách mà.

exercises.question: He has ______ many followers ______ his posts reach millions.
exercises.explanation: So many...that - mệnh đề kết quả.

exercises.question: The journalist spoke ______ he had witnessed the event.
exercises.explanation: As though - mệnh đề cách thức với Past Perfect.

exercises.question: It was ______ viral video ______ everyone shared it.
exercises.explanation: Such a...that - mệnh đề kết quả với danh từ đếm được.

exercises.question: Information spreads ______ it happened.
exercises.explanation: As - mệnh đề cách thức: như, theo cách (thực tế).

exercises.question_text: After 'as if' and 'as though', we often use past tenses to show unreal situations.
exercises.explanation: Đúng. As if/as though thường dùng thì quá khứ để chỉ tình huống không có thật.

exercises.question_text: 'So' is used with adjectives and adverbs, while 'such' is used with nouns.
exercises.explanation: Đúng. So + adj/adv, such + noun phrase.

exercises.question_text: We can say 'so a good journalist' in result clauses.
exercises.explanation: Sai. Phải dùng 'such a good journalist'.

exercises.question_text: 'The way' can be used to introduce manner clauses.
exercises.explanation: Đúng. 'The way' dùng để giới thiệu mệnh đề cách thức.

exercises.question_text: Mass media can influence public opinion.
exercises.explanation: Đúng. Truyền thông đại chúng có thể ảnh hưởng đến dư luận.

exercises.question_text: What do we call false information spread online? (two words)
exercises.explanation: Fake news: tin giả.

exercises.question_text: Complete: The news was ______ shocking ______ everyone talked about it.
exercises.explanation: So...that - mệnh đề kết quả.

exercises.question_text: Complete: She talks ______ she knew everything.
exercises.explanation: As if/as though - mệnh đề cách thức.

exercises.question_text: What do we call content that spreads rapidly online? (one word)
exercises.explanation: Viral: lan truyền nhanh.

exercises.question_text: Write a short paragraph (80-100 words) about the influence of social media. Use adverbial clauses of result.
exercises.sample_answer: Social media has become so influential that it affects nearly every aspect of modern life. Platforms like Facebook and Instagram are so popular that billions of people use them daily. Information spreads so quickly on social media that news can reach millions within minutes. However, this rapid spread has such negative consequences that fake news and misinformation have become serious problems. Social media influencers have so many followers that they can shape public opinion and consumer behavior. The impact is so significant that governments are developing regulations to address privacy concerns. Despite the challenges, social media remains such an important communication tool that most people cannot imagine life without it.
exercises.explanation: Bài viết sử dụng nhiều mệnh đề kết quả: so influential that, so popular that, so quickly that, such negative consequences that, so many followers that, so significant that, such an important tool that.

exercises.question_text: Write a short paragraph (80-100 words) about fake news. Use adverbial clauses of manner and result.
exercises.sample_answer: Fake news has become such a serious problem that it threatens democracy and public trust. Some people share false information as if it were verified fact, without checking sources. Misinformation spreads so rapidly on social media that millions can see it before fact-checkers identify it as false. Some news outlets report stories as though they were objective, but they actually have political bias. The situation is so concerning that many countries are implementing media literacy programs in schools. We should not believe everything we read as if it were automatically true. Instead, we need to verify information from multiple reliable sources. The challenge is so great that everyone must develop critical thinking skills.
exercises.explanation: Bài viết kết hợp mệnh đề cách thức (as if it were, as though they were) và mệnh đề kết quả (such a serious problem that, so rapidly that, so concerning that, so great that).

exercises.question_text: Write a short paragraph (80-100 words) about how you consume news and media. Use adverbial clauses.
exercises.sample_answer: I consume news and media in ways that reflect modern habits. I get most of my information from online sources because digital media is so convenient that I can access it anytime on my phone. I follow several news websites and social media accounts, but I'm careful to check multiple sources as journalists advise. Some posts spread so quickly that I wonder if they're accurate, so I verify important news before sharing. I watch TV news occasionally, though it feels as if traditional media is becoming less relevant to young people. Social media algorithms show me content in such a personalized way that I sometimes worry about living in an information bubble. Therefore, I make an effort to read diverse perspectives.
exercises.explanation: Bài viết sử dụng cả mệnh đề cách thức (as journalists advise, as if traditional media is, in such a way that) và mệnh đề kết quả (so convenient that, so quickly that).

exercises.question_text: Write a short paragraph (80-100 words) comparing traditional and digital media. Use adverbial clauses.
exercises.sample_answer: Traditional and digital media serve the same purpose but function in such different ways that they appeal to different audiences. Traditional media like newspapers and TV report news as they always have, with editorial oversight and fact-checking. However, digital media spreads information so quickly that it often reaches audiences before traditional outlets can publish. Social media is so interactive that users can comment and share instantly, while traditional media operates as if communication were one-way. Print newspapers are declining so rapidly that many have closed or moved online. Yet traditional media maintains such credibility that people still trust established news organizations more than social media sources. The future likely involves both, as each has strengths the other lacks.
exercises.explanation: Bài viết kết hợp mệnh đề cách thức (as they always have, as if communication were) và mệnh đề kết quả (in such different ways that, so quickly that, so interactive that, so rapidly that, such credibility that).
